product.name: Leadtype
product.summary: A docs pipeline that turns one MDX source into a website, agent-readable artifacts, and a search index.

pages.title: Leadtype
pages.description: One MDX source. Hosted docs artifacts, package-bundled AGENTS.md, and search output from the same pipeline.

pages.title: Collections
pages.description: Declare multi-source docs sets with defineCollection — local folders, git repos, and per-collection schemas.

pages.title: Components
pages.description: MDX components the pipeline knows how to flatten into agent-readable markdown.

pages.title: Frontmatter
pages.description: Required fields, group semantics, and how authored MDX becomes a navigation tree.

pages.title: Add search
pages.description: Generate a static docs search index, query it at runtime, and optionally stream source-grounded answers.

pages.title: Build a docs site
pages.description: Pick the right leadtype integration shape for your docs app, and what each path gives you.

pages.title: Framework integration matrix
pages.description: Use Leadtype with native-feeling recipes for Next, Nuxt, Astro, SvelteKit, TanStack Start, and Fumadocs.

pages.title: Generate static artifacts
pages.description: Run leadtype generate from your build pipeline to write llms.txt, markdown mirrors, search index, sitemap, and agent-readability files to disk.

pages.title: Integrate with Fumadocs
pages.description: Wire leadtype's content layer into a fumadocs app for nav, search, and includes.

pages.title: Optimize docs for agents
pages.description: Set up llms.txt, markdown mirrors, JSON-LD, sitemaps, robots.txt, and audit checks for an agent-readable docs site.

pages.title: Use the source primitive
pages.description: Wire createDocsSource into Next, Astro, Vite, Nuxt, SvelteKit, or any MDX-aware bundler. Same primitive, multiple host shapes.

pages.title: Validate in CI
pages.description: Run leadtype lint in CI so frontmatter, navigation, and link issues fail PRs before publish.

pages.title: How it works
pages.description: The mental model: one MDX source, a remark pipeline, two output modes, three audiences.

pages.title: Methodology
pages.description: How leadtype differs from Fumadocs, Starlight, and Mintlify.

pages.title: Bundle docs into a package
pages.description: Ship agent-readable docs inside an npm tarball — AGENTS.md at the package root plus per-topic .md files.

pages.title: Quickstart
pages.description: Install leadtype, author your first MDX page, and create a source. Then plug it into whatever framework you're using.

pages.title: Architecture
pages.description: The core / adapter boundary — what ships where, and the rules adapters must follow.

pages.title: CLI
pages.description: leadtype generate, leadtype sync, and leadtype lint — flags, exit codes, and JSON output.

pages.title: Convert
pages.description: MDX-to-markdown conversion APIs from leadtype/convert.

pages.title: Evals
pages.description: How Leadtype benchmarks AGENTS.md, llms.txt, and llms-full.txt output before changing defaults.

pages.title: Lint rules
pages.description: Schema, link, and navigation checks. CLI and library API.

pages.title: LLM files
pages.description: Generate llms.txt for hosted websites and AGENTS.md for npm-bundled offline reading.

pages.title: leadtype/mdx
pages.description: Tag type contracts and the build-time source preset for consumers rendering MDX themselves.

pages.title: Remark plugins
pages.description: The default plugin stack that flattens MDX components into markdown.

pages.title: Search
pages.description: Static search index, runtime helpers, and source-grounded answer streaming.

pages.title: createDocsSource
pages.description: Framework-neutral docs source primitive — navigation, page loader, search index, and include resolver.
